mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in the unemployment rate above 3.5% over 1 month signals deteriorating labor market conditions, which weakens corporate earnings expectations and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite rising UNRATE, or Rank IC < 0.02 over 1-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in industrial production and rise in inflation expectations creates a stagflationary environment that depresses equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls > 1.5% in 2 months AND CPIAUCSL rises > 0.5pp in 1 month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of instances where both INDPRO declines and CPIAUCSL rises, or joint signal IC < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A recovery in housing starts above 1,500,000 units signals improved consumer confidence and business investment, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 40% of triggered instances despite HOUST growth, or Rank IC < 0.04 over 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in inflation expectations above 2.5% triggers central bank hawkishness, leading to higher real interest rates that depress equity valuations and generate negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 55% of instances where CPIAUCSL rises, or IC < 0.03 over 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A strong payroll growth above 150,000 jobs signals robust labor market conditions that support corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 45% of triggered instances despite PAYEMS growth, or IC < 0.02 over 1-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in real private sector credit (RSAFS) below 520,000 million dollars signals reduced business investment, which weakens corporate earnings and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of instances where RSAFS declines, or IC < 0.04 over 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production above 109.2 index points indicates expanding manufacturing activity, which boosts corporate profits and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 40% of triggered instances despite INDPRO growth, or IC < 0.05 over 2-year window.
